channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan's CDF Munir visits Tehran to advance Iran-US peace negotiations
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, has left for Tehran on an official visit amid Pakistan's active role in mediating between the United States an...

item.title: AI Tools Helping Kenyan Traders Make Smarter Forex Decisions
item.description: In Nairobi and across Kenya, artificial intelligence (AI) is steadily transforming how forex trading is done. In the past, only hedge funds in major financial hubs like New York had access to sophisti...

item.title: Underground abortion networks grow in Dar es Salaam amid strict legal restrictions
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, strict laws against abortion have pushed many women into a hidden and risky underground market. This informal network involves brokers, pharmacies, and unregistered providers who ope...

item.title: Eastern Cape Remains South Africa’s Most Murder-Prone Province Despite Drop in National Crime
item.description: Despite a general decline in violent crime across South Africa, the Eastern Cape continues to have the highest murder rate in the country, with 14.3 murders per 100,000 people, nearly double that of G...

item.title: Tanzania launches national waste recycling program to protect environment and generate youth employment
item.description: Tanzania imeanza mpango mkubwa wa kuendeleza sekta ya kuchakata taka ili kupunguza mabadiliko ya tabianchi na pia kutoa ajira kwa vijana wengi. Serikali imetangaza mpango wa kitaifa wa kuchakata taka ...

item.title: Rodrigo Duterte to remain in ICC detention pending trial for crimes against humanity
item.description: Former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte will continue to be held in detention at The Hague as he awaits the start of his trial at the International Criminal Court (ICC) over allegations of crimes ...

item.title: Tim Hortons Announces $43 Million Renovation Project for Vancouver Island Stores
item.description: Tim Hortons has announced a significant $43 million investment aimed at renovating its Vancouver Island locations. The initiative is part of the chain's broader commitment to modernizing its presence ...

item.title: Mobile Surgical Unit Brings Specialist Healthcare Closer to Communities
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania has taken a big step to improve healthcare access for wananchi in remote and underserved areas with the launch of the country's first mobile surgical theatre by Muhimbili Nati...

item.title: Brig. Gen. Randulf Tuaño assumes leadership of Western Visayas police
item.description: Brigadier General Randulf Tuaño, previously the spokesman of the Philippine National Police (PNP), has officially been appointed as the new police chief of Western Visayas. He succeeds Brig. Gen. Jose...

item.title: Strategies for Pierre Poilievre’s Potential Political Comeback
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre faces a significant challenge in regaining the prime ministership after his party's recent election loss, despite previously leading in the polls. Analysts suggest...

item.title: The Use of Canaries as Biological Early Warning Systems in Coal Mines
item.description: The use of canaries in coal mines started in the late 19th century as a life-saving measure against invisible toxic gases. British scientist John Scott Haldane proposed the idea in 1895 after investig...

item.title: Lupita Nyong’o Talks About Her Role and Diversity in The Odyssey
item.description: Kenyan-Mexican actress Lupita Nyong’o has responded to the debates around her casting in Christopher Nolan’s upcoming film The Odyssey. She plays the dual roles of Helen of Troy and Clytemnestra, whic...

item.title: Kevin Warsh assumes role as Federal Reserve chairman amid market uncertainty
item.description: Kevin Warsh has officially been sworn in as the chairman of the US Federal Reserve, signalling a shift towards a 'reform-oriented' leadership approach. Financial markets are anticipating an interest-r...

item.title: Quebec Coroner Highlights Need for Swimming Lessons After Mother and Son Drown
item.description: A tragic incident in Montreal last June saw a 34-year-old mother and her three-year-old son drown while at their home pool. According to a report by Quebec coroner Edgard Nassif, the child fell into t...

item.title: Chandigarh Man Arrested for Cruelty After Throwing Puppy into Hot Tandoor
item.description: In a shocking incident from Chandigarh, a man identified as Niraj alias Bhola has been arrested for allegedly throwing a live puppy into a burning tandoor outside a dhaba in the Bapu Dham area. The in...

item.title: British Army conducts secret Nato exercise in London Underground highlighting drone shortfall
item.description: Deep within the disused terminus of Charing Cross station, a secret Nato command bunker has hosted a British-led military exercise simulating a Russian attack on Estonia in 2030. Known as Arrcade Stri...

item.title: New Balance’s Grey Days Campaign Captures Johannesburg Winter Style
item.description: As Johannesburg settles into winter, New Balance’s Grey Days campaign has struck a chord with the city’s seasonal fashion mood. Winter in Jozi encourages thoughtful layering, stylish comfort, and a fo...

item.title: Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie’s 2006 Motorcycle Visit to Ho Chi Minh City
item.description: In November 2006, Hollywood stars Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie visited Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, in a surprising and low-key manner that contrasted sharply with typical celebrity trips. Flying in fro...

item.title: Guidelines and Risks for Traveling with Medical Marijuana by Air
item.description: Traveling with medical marijuana in the U.S. has become somewhat more legally recognized, but complications remain. The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) recently updated its website to ref...

item.title: American Skateboarder Jason Vanporppal Nears Completion of Africa-Wide Skateboarding Journey for Charity
item.description: American skateboarder Jason Vanporppal is close to completing a remarkable journey from Kampala, Uganda to Cape Town, South Africa, covering over 6,400 kilometres across seven African countries. Begin...
